source.name: Campus France — Proof of financial means for a student visa

requirements.label: Acceptance from a French institution
requirements.detail: An admission/enrolment letter from a recognised French higher-education institution.

requirements.label: Études en France procedure (nationality-dependent)
requirements.detail: Applicants from countries connected to the 'Études en France' platform must complete it via Campus France before the visa.

requirements.label: Proof of financial means
requirements.detail: Show at least €615 per month for your stay (about €7,380 for a 10-month year); Paris consulates often advise more.

requirements.label: Health insurance
requirements.detail: Arrange cover for arrival; most students then register with France's student health system.

requirements.label: Language proof
requirements.detail: French (DELF/DALF/TCF) for French-taught programmes, or IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught.

requirements.label: Valid passport
requirements.detail: A passport valid for the duration of the long-stay visa (VLS-TS).

requirements.label: Proof of accommodation
requirements.detail: Evidence of where you'll live in France is usually requested.

requirements.label: Tuition / funds transfer
requirements.detail: Transfer fees or living funds with a low-FX provider.

requirements.label: Connectivity on arrival
requirements.detail: An eSIM gives you a French number and data on landing.
